award_info.name: Giải Pulitzer cho Tiểu thuyết
award_info.description: Giải thưởng văn học danh giá của Mỹ, công nhận tác phẩm tiểu thuyết xuất sắc của tác giả Mỹ, ưu tiên cho tác phẩm về đời sống người Mỹ
award_info.notes: Từ 1918-1947 gọi là 'Pulitzer Prize for the Novel'. Từ 1948 đổi tên thành 'Pulitzer Prize for Fiction' và mở rộng cho cả truyện ngắn, novella

laureates.name: Percival Everett
laureates.citation: An accomplished reconsideration of 'Huckleberry Finn' that gives agency to Jim to illustrate the absurdity of racial supremacy and provide a new take on the search for family and freedom

laureates.name: Jayne Anne Phillips
laureates.citation: A beautifully rendered novel set in West Virginia's Trans-Allegheny Lunatic Asylum in the aftermath of the Civil War, where a severely wounded Union veteran, a 12-year-old girl and her mother, long abused by a Confederate soldier, struggle to heal

laureates.name: Hernan Diaz
laureates.citation: A riveting novel set in a bygone America that explores family, wealth and ambition through linked narratives rendered in different literary styles, a complex examination of love and power in a country where capitalism is king

laureates.name: Barbara Kingsolver
laureates.citation: A masterful recasting of 'David Copperfield,' narrated by an Appalachian boy whose wise, unwavering voice relates his encounters with poverty, addiction, institutional failures and moral collapse–and his efforts to conquer them

2023.notes: First time in Pulitzer Fiction history that two winners were named

laureates.name: Joshua Cohen

work.title: The Netanyahus: An Account of a Minor and Ultimately Even Negligible Episode in the History of a Very Famous Family

laureates.citation: A mordant, linguistically deft historical novel about the ambiguities of the Jewish American experience, presenting ideas and disputes as volatile as its tightly-wound plot

laureates.name: Louise Erdrich
laureates.citation: A majestic, polyphonic novel about a community's efforts to halt the proposed displacement and elimination of several Native American tribes in the 1950s, rendered with dexterity and imagination

laureates.name: Colson Whitehead
laureates.citation: A spare and devastating exploration of abuse at a reform school in Jim Crow-era Florida that is ultimately a powerful tale of human perseverance, dignity and redemption
laureates.notes: Second Pulitzer Prize for Colson Whitehead

laureates.name: Richard Powers
laureates.citation: An ingeniously structured narrative that branches and canopies like the trees at the core of the story whose wonder and connectivity echo those of the humans living amongst them

laureates.name: Andrew Sean Greer
laureates.citation: A generous book, musical in its prose and expansive in its structure and range, about growing older and the essential nature of love

laureates.name: Colson Whitehead
laureates.citation: For a smart melding of realism and allegory that combines the violence of slavery and the drama of escape in a myth that speaks to contemporary America
laureates.notes: First Pulitzer Prize for Colson Whitehead

laureates.name: Viet Thanh Nguyen
laureates.birth_state: California (born in Buôn Ma Thuột, Vietnam)
laureates.citation: A layered immigrant tale told in the wry, confessional voice of a 'man of two minds' -- and two countries, Vietnam and the United States

laureates.name: Anthony Doerr

work.title: All the Light We Cannot See

laureates.citation: An imaginative and intricate novel inspired by the horrors of World War II and written in short, elegant chapters that explore human nature and the contradictory power of technology

laureates.name: Donna Tartt
laureates.citation: A beautifully written coming-of-age novel with exquisitely drawn characters that follows a grieving boy's entanglement with a small famous painting that has eluded destruction, a book that stimulates the mind and touches the heart

laureates.name: Adam Johnson
laureates.citation: An exquisitely crafted novel that carries the reader on an adventuresome journey into the depths of totalitarian North Korea and into the most intimate spaces of the human heart

2012.notes: No award was given this year. Finalists were: Karen Russell (Swamplandia!), David Foster Wallace (The Pale King, posthumously), Denis Johnson (Train Dreams)

laureates.name: Jennifer Egan

work.title: A Visit from the Goon Squad

laureates.citation: An inventive investigation of growing up and growing old in the digital age, displaying a big-hearted curiosity about cultural change at warp speed

laureates.name: Paul Harding
laureates.citation: A powerful celebration of life in which a New England father and son, through suffering and joy, transcend their imprisoning lives and offer new ways of perceiving the world and mortality

laureates.name: Elizabeth Strout
laureates.citation: A collection of 13 short stories set in small-town Maine that packs a cumulative emotional wallop, bound together by polished prose and by Olive, the title character, blunt, flawed and fascinating

laureates.name: Junot Díaz

work.title: The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao

laureates.birth_state: New Jersey (born in Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic)

work.title: The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay

laureates.name: Jhumpa Lahiri
laureates.birth_state: Rhode Island (born in London, United Kingdom)

work.title: Martin Dressler: The Tale of an American Dreamer
A Good Scent from a Strange Mountain

laureates.name: John Updike
laureates.notes: Second Pulitzer Prize for John Updike

work.title: The Mambo Kings Play Songs of Love

laureates.name: John Updike
laureates.notes: First Pulitzer Prize for John Updike

1977.notes: No award was given this year. The jury recommended 'A River Runs Through It' by Norman MacLean, but the Pulitzer board made no award

1974.notes: No award was given this year. The jury unanimously recommended 'Gravity's Rainbow' by Thomas Pynchon, but the Pulitzer board made no award

1971.notes: No award was given this year. The jury put forth three novels for consideration but the board rejected all three

1964.notes: No award was given this year. The jury could not recommend any 1963 novel as 'distinguished fiction'

laureates.name: William Faulkner
laureates.notes: Posthumously awarded. Second Pulitzer Prize for William Faulkner

1957.notes: No award was given this year. The jury recommended 'The Voice at the Back Door' by Elizabeth Spencer, but the Pulitzer board chose to give no award

laureates.name: William Faulkner
laureates.notes: First Pulitzer Prize for William Faulkner

1954.notes: No award was given this year. The two-man jury could not agree on a single book to recommend

work.title: The Old Man and the Sea

laureates.name: James A. Michener
laureates.notes: First time a short story collection won under the new 'Fiction' category name

1946.notes: No award was given this year. The jury could not reach consensus; one point of contention was whether 'Black Boy' by Richard Wright qualified as a novel or memoir

1941.notes: No award was given this year. The jury recommended 'For Whom the Bell Tolls' by Ernest Hemingway, but Columbia University president Nicholas Murray Butler persuaded the board to reverse its judgment because he deemed the novel offensive

work.title: The Bridge of San Luis Rey

laureates.name: Booth Tarkington
laureates.notes: Second Pulitzer Prize for Booth Tarkington

laureates.name: Edith Wharton
laureates.notes: The jury actually preferred 'Main Street' by Sinclair Lewis, but the Pulitzer board overturned their decision

1920.notes: No award was given this year due to a dispute over the wording of the award criteria ('whole' vs 'wholesome' atmosphere of American life)

laureates.name: Booth Tarkington
laureates.notes: First Pulitzer Prize for Booth Tarkington

laureates.name: Ernest Poole
laureates.notes: First Pulitzer Prize for Fiction ever awarded
